driver_subject: netdevsim: rely on XFRM state direction instead of flags
kernel_subject: xfrm: store and rely on direction to construct offload flags
stage1_reason: Driver changes adapt to new kernel data types (tos→dscp_t in structs) and XFRM direction API (flags→dir).
stage2_reason: Driver commit '55e2f83afb1c' explicitly adapts to XFRM direction API, matching kernel commit '482db2f1dd21' which introduces XFRM_DEV_OFFLOAD_IN and changes from flags to direction. Driver commits '20bbf32efe1e', '568a3f33b427', '888ade8f90d7' directly correspond to kernel commits '568a3f33b427' and '888ade8f90d7' (same hashes) that change to dscp_t in fib structs.
